question_text: Which of the following best explains why different foods have different energy values (measured in kJ or kcal) even if they have the same mass?

options.label: A
options.text: Different foods contain different amounts of chemical energy in their molecular bonds; fats store more energy per gram than carbohydrates or proteins

options.label: B
options.text: Foods with stronger colors contain more energy than foods with weaker colors

options.label: C
options.text: The digestive system breaks down different foods at different speeds, so faster digestion means more energy

options.label: D
options.text: Foods grown in different climates have different energy values regardless of their composition

explanation: Different foods store energy differently in their chemical bonds. Fats store approximately 39 kJ per gram, while carbohydrates and proteins store about 17 kJ per gram. So a 100g portion of butter contains much more energy than 100g of carrot, even though they weigh the same.
